en.tldr: This paper provides a compact yet rigorous introduction to second-order optimality conditions for MPECs, covering three main viewpoints.
en.motivation: Classical second-order conditions fail under equilibrium constraints, necessitating a specialized framework.
en.method: Starting from the classical NLP template, it explains the failure and develops multiplier-based, implicit-programming, and piecewise-programming conditions.
en.result: Clarifies the roles of critical cones, strong regularity, and curvature terms.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers quickly grasp the core concepts and different perspectives of MPEC second-order optimality conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Combine implicit-programming conditions with implicit layers in deep learning to design differentiable equilibrium layers.
idea_spark.risk: Implicit-programming conditions rely on differentiability of the solution map, which may be hard to guarantee in practice.
idea_spark.inspiration: The concept of critical cones inspires more efficient constraint screening strategies.

papers.title: Adaptation and Development of Super Schemes for Unconstrained Optimization Problems
papers.summary: In this paper, we propose a class of super-schemes for efficiently solving nonlinear unconstrained optimization problems. The proposed approach introduces two novel choices of step-size parameters, leading to efficient descent directions without requiring second-order information. We develop one-step, two-step, and three-step iterative schemes (denoted by SS1, SS2, and SS3) and establish that these methods achieve higher-order convergence of orders two, four, and six, respectively. Despite their high convergence rates, the computational complexity of the proposed methods remains comparable to existing gradient-based methods, with a cost of $\mathcal{O}(n^2)$ per iteration. The proposed methods are simple to implement and do not require complicated line-search procedures. Their effectiveness is demonstrated through extensive numerical experiments on a wide range of problems, including large-scale and ill-conditioned cases. The results show that the proposed methods significantly outperform classical methods, such as the Barzilai-Borwein method and other gradient-based approaches, in terms of iteration count and computational efficiency. Finally, the numerical results are consistent with the theoretical analysis, confirming the stability of the proposed schemes for test optimization problems.

ai.tldr: 提出一类超方案，通过两种新步长参数实现高效下降方向，无需二阶信息，达到二阶、四阶和六阶收敛，计算复杂度与梯度法相当。
ai.motivation: 现有梯度法收敛慢，需要二阶信息或复杂线搜索，本文旨在设计简单高效的高阶收敛方法。
ai.method: 引入两种新步长参数，构造一步、两步和三步迭代格式（SS1、SS2、SS3），分别达到二阶、四阶和六阶收敛，每步计算量为O(n²)。
ai.result: 在广泛测试问题（包括大规模和病态问题）上，所提方法在迭代次数和计算效率上显著优于Barzilai-Borwein等经典梯度法，数值结果与理论分析一致。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需二阶信息、无需线搜索的高阶收敛优化算法，适合大规模无约束优化问题。

idea_spark.idea: 将超方案与自适应步长或随机梯度结合，用于机器学习中的大规模参数优化。
idea_spark.risk: 高阶收敛可能对问题条件数敏感，在高度病态问题上稳定性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Barzilai-Borwein方法启发，通过多步信息加速梯度下降，类似思想可推广到深度学习优化器设计。

en.tldr: Proposes a class of super schemes with two novel step-size parameters for unconstrained optimization, achieving second-, fourth-, and sixth-order convergence without second-order information, with O(n²) cost per iteration.
en.motivation: Existing gradient methods converge slowly or require second-order information or complex line searches; the goal is to design simple, high-order convergent methods.
en.method: Introduces two new step-size parameters to construct one-step, two-step, and three-step iterative schemes (SS1, SS2, SS3) achieving second-, fourth-, and sixth-order convergence, each with O(n²) computational cost.
en.result: On a wide range of test problems (including large-scale and ill-conditioned cases), the proposed methods significantly outperform classical gradient methods like Barzilai-Borwein in iteration count and computational efficiency; numerical results confirm theoretical analysis.
en.help_to_user: Provides high-order convergent optimization algorithms that require no second-order information or line search, suitable for large-scale unconstrained problems.

idea_spark.idea: Combine super schemes with adaptive step sizes or stochastic gradients for large-scale parameter optimization in machine learning.
idea_spark.risk: High-order convergence may be sensitive to problem conditioning; stability on highly ill-conditioned problems needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Barzilai-Borwein methods, using multi-step information to accelerate gradient descent; similar ideas could be extended to deep learning optimizer design.

papers.title: WFM: 3D Wavelet Flow Matching for Ultrafast Multi-Modal MRI Synthesis
papers.summary: Diffusion models have achieved remarkable quality in multi-modal MRI synthesis, but their computational cost (hundreds of sampling steps and separate models per modality) limits clinical deployment. We observe that this inefficiency stems from an unnecessary starting point: diffusion begins from pure noise, discarding the structural information already present in available MRI sequences. We propose WFM (Wavelet Flow Matching), which instead learns a direct flow from an informed prior, the mean of conditioning modalities in wavelet space, to the target distribution. Because the source and target share underlying anatomy and differ primarily in contrast, this formulation enables accurate synthesis in just 1-2 integration steps. A single 82M-parameter model with class conditioning synthesizes all four BraTS modalities (T1, T1c, T2, FLAIR), replacing four separate diffusion models totaling 326M parameters. On BraTS 2024, WFM achieves 26.8 dB PSNR and 0.94 SSIM, within 1-2 dB of diffusion baselines, while running 250-1000x faster (0.16-0.64s vs. 160s per volume). This speed-quality trade-off makes real-time MRI synthesis practical for clinical workflows. Code is available at https://github.com/yalcintur/WFM.

ai.tldr: 提出WFM，利用小波空间的条件模态均值作为先验，实现1-2步超快速多模态MRI合成，速度提升250-1000倍。
ai.motivation: 扩散模型在多模态MRI合成中计算成本高（数百步采样、每模态独立模型），且从纯噪声出发浪费了已有MRI序列的结构信息。
ai.method: 学习从小波空间中条件模态的均值到目标分布的直交流，单模型（82M参数）通过类别条件合成所有四种BraTS模态。
ai.result: 在BraTS 2024上PSNR 26.8 dB、SSIM 0.94，与扩散基线相差1-2 dB，但速度从160秒/体积降至0.16-0.64秒/体积。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种极低延迟的MRI合成方案，适合临床实时应用，并开源代码。

idea_spark.idea: 利用多模态数据中共享的解剖结构作为先验，设计更高效的生成路径。
idea_spark.risk: 合成质量在极端对比度差异或噪声场景下可能下降，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从“从零开始”的扩散转向“从已有信息出发”的流匹配，启发其他生成任务中先验的设计。

en.tldr: WFM uses the mean of conditioning modalities in wavelet space as an informed prior to achieve 1-2 step ultrafast multi-modal MRI synthesis, with 250-1000x speedup.
en.motivation: Diffusion models for multi-modal MRI synthesis are computationally expensive (hundreds of steps, separate models per modality) and waste structural information by starting from pure noise.
en.method: Learn a direct flow from the mean of conditioning modalities in wavelet space to the target distribution; a single 82M-parameter model with class conditioning synthesizes all four BraTS modalities.
en.result: On BraTS 2024, achieves 26.8 dB PSNR and 0.94 SSIM, within 1-2 dB of diffusion baselines, while reducing runtime from 160s to 0.16-0.64s per volume.
en.help_to_user: Provides a low-latency MRI synthesis method suitable for clinical real-time use, with open-source code.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage shared anatomy in multi-modal data as a prior to design more efficient generation paths.
idea_spark.risk: Synthesis quality may degrade under extreme contrast differences or noisy conditions; generalization needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Shifting from 'starting from scratch' diffusion to 'starting from available information' flow matching inspires prior design in other generative tasks.

papers.title: Phonological Subspace Collapse Is Aetiology-Specific and Cross-Lingually Stable: Evidence from 3,374 Speakers
papers.summary: We previously introduced a training-free method for dysarthria severity assessment based on d-prime separability of phonological feature subspaces in frozen self-supervised speech representations, validated on 890 speakers across 5 languages with HuBERT-base. Here, we scale the analysis to 3,374 speakers from 25 datasets spanning 12 languages and 5 aetiologies (Parkinson's disease, cerebral palsy, ALS, Down syndrome, and stroke), plus healthy controls, using 6 SSL backbones. We report three findings. First, aetiology-specific degradation profiles are distinguishable at the group level: 10 of 13 features yield large effect sizes (epsilon-squared > 0.14, Holm-corrected p < 0.001), with Parkinson's disease separable from the articulatory execution group at Cohen's d = 0.83; individual-level classification remains limited (22.6% macro F1). Second, profiles show cross-lingual profile-shape stability: cosine similarity of 5-dimensional consonant d-prime profiles exceeds 0.95 across the languages available for each aetiology. Absolute d-prime magnitudes are not cross-lingually calibrated, so the method supports language-independent phenotyping of degradation patterns but requires within-corpus calibration for absolute severity interpretation. Third, the method is architecture-independent: all 6 backbones produce monotonic severity gradients with inter-model agreement exceeding rho = 0.77. Fixed-token d-prime estimation preserves the severity correlation (rho = -0.733 at 200 tokens per class), confirming that the signal is not a token-count artefact. These results support phonological subspace analysis as a robust, training-free framework for aetiology-aware dysarthria characterisation, with evidence of cross-lingual profile-shape stability and cross-backbone robustness in the represented sample.

ai.tldr: 基于3,374名说话人的大规模分析，验证了音位子空间坍塌在病因特异性和跨语言稳定性方面的鲁棒性，支持无训练构音障碍评估。
ai.motivation: 此前方法在小规模数据上有效，但需要在大规模、多病因、多语言条件下验证其泛化能力。
ai.method: 使用6种自监督语音骨干网络，对3,374名说话人（25个数据集、12种语言、5种病因）提取音位特征子空间的d-prime可分离性，进行组间比较和跨语言一致性分析。
ai.result: 10/13特征效应量大（ε²>0.14），帕金森病与执行性构音障碍组Cohen's d=0.83；跨语言轮廓余弦相似度>0.95；不同骨干网络间严重度梯度单调且一致性ρ>0.77。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种无需训练、跨语言稳定的构音障碍表型分析方法，可用于临床辅助诊断和语言无关的病理模式识别。

idea_spark.idea: 结合音位子空间轮廓与个体级分类器（如轻量级神经网络），可能提升个体诊断精度。
idea_spark.risk: 绝对d-prime值跨语言不可校准，需在语料库内标准化；个体级分类性能仍有限（宏F1=22.6%）。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用自监督表示中音位子空间的几何结构，可设计跨语言、跨病因的通用语音健康评估工具。

zh.result: 10/13特征效应量大（ε²>0.14），帕金森病与执行性构音障碍组Cohen's d=0.83；跨语言轮廓余弦相似度>0.95；不同骨干网络间严重度梯度单调且一致性ρ>0.77。

idea_spark.risk: 绝对d-prime值跨语言不可校准，需在语料库内标准化；个体级分类性能仍有限（宏F1=22.6%）。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用自监督表示中音位子空间的几何结构，可设计跨语言、跨病因的通用语音健康评估工具。

en.tldr: A large-scale analysis of 3,374 speakers validates that phonological subspace collapse is aetiology-specific and cross-lingually stable, supporting training-free dysarthria assessment.
en.motivation: Previous method worked on small data; needed validation across diverse aetiologies, languages, and backbones.
en.method: Extracted d-prime separability of phonological feature subspaces from 6 SSL backbones for 3,374 speakers (25 datasets, 12 languages, 5 aetiologies), comparing group profiles and cross-lingual consistency.
en.result: 10/13 features showed large effect sizes (ε²>0.14); Parkinson's vs. articulatory execution group Cohen's d=0.83; cross-lingual profile cosine similarity >0.95; all backbones yielded monotonic severity gradients with inter-model agreement ρ>0.77.
en.help_to_user: Provides a training-free, cross-lingually stable framework for dysarthria phenotyping, useful for clinical screening and language-independent pathology characterization.

idea_spark.idea: Combining phonological subspace profiles with lightweight individual-level classifiers may improve diagnostic accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Absolute d-prime values are not cross-lingually calibrated; individual-level classification remains limited (macro F1=22.6%).
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging geometric structure of phonological subspaces in SSL representations can inspire universal speech health assessment tools across languages and aetiologies.

papers.title: Decoupled DiLoCo for Resilient Distributed Pre-training
papers.summary: Modern large-scale language model pre-training relies heavily on the single program multiple data (SPMD) paradigm, which requires tight coupling across accelerators. Due to this coupling, transient slowdowns, hardware failures, and synchronization overhead stall the entire computation, wasting significant compute time at scale. While recent distributed methods like DiLoCo reduced communication bandwidth, they remained fundamentally synchronous and vulnerable to these system stalls. To address this, we introduce Decoupled DiLoCo, an evolution of the DiLoCo framework designed to break the lock-step synchronization barrier and go beyond SPMD to maximize training goodput. Decoupled DiLoCo partitions compute across multiple independent ``learners'' that execute local inner optimization steps. These learners asynchronously communicate parameter fragments to a central synchronizer, which circumvents failed or straggling learners by aggregating updates using a minimum quorum, an adaptive grace window, and dynamic token-weighted merging. Inspired by ``chaos engineering'', we achieve significantly improved training efficiency in failure-prone environments with millions of simulated chips with strictly zero global downtime, while maintaining competitive model performance across text and vision tasks, for both dense and mixture-of-expert architectures.

ai.tldr: 提出Decoupled DiLoCo框架，通过解耦同步屏障和异步通信，在故障环境中实现零全局停机的高效分布式预训练。
ai.motivation: 现有SPMD范式下，加速器间的紧密耦合导致瞬态降速、硬件故障和同步开销会阻塞整个计算，浪费大量算力。
ai.method: 将计算划分为多个独立学习者，每个执行本地内优化步骤；学习者异步向中央同步器发送参数片段，同步器通过最小法定人数、自适应宽限期和动态令牌加权合并来规避故障或掉队者。
ai.result: 在模拟数百万芯片的故障环境中，实现严格零全局停机，训练效率显著提升，并在文本和视觉任务上保持有竞争力的模型性能，适用于密集和混合专家架构。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过异步解耦和容错机制提升大规模分布式训练的鲁棒性和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合混沌工程原则，设计自适应宽限期和动态加权合并策略，可进一步探索在异构硬件或动态网络环境下的自适应优化。
idea_spark.risk: 异步更新可能导致模型收敛不稳定或精度损失，需要仔细调优法定人数和合并策略。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从混沌工程中获得灵感，主动注入故障并设计容错机制，启发在更多AI系统中采用类似方法提升可靠性。

zh.tldr: 提出Decoupled DiLoCo框架，通过解耦同步屏障和异步通信，在故障环境中实现零全局停机的高效分布式预训练。

idea_spark.idea: 结合混沌工程原则，设计自适应宽限期和动态加权合并策略，可进一步探索在异构硬件或动态网络环境下的自适应优化。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从混沌工程中获得灵感，主动注入故障并设计容错机制，启发在更多AI系统中采用类似方法提升可靠性。

en.tldr: Proposes Decoupled DiLoCo, a framework that breaks synchronous barriers and uses asynchronous communication to achieve zero global downtime in failure-prone distributed pre-training.
en.motivation: The tight coupling in SPMD paradigm causes transient slowdowns, hardware failures, and synchronization overhead to stall entire computation, wasting significant compute at scale.
en.method: Partitions compute into independent learners executing local inner optimization steps; learners asynchronously send parameter fragments to a central synchronizer, which uses a minimum quorum, adaptive grace window, and dynamic token-weighted merging to circumvent failed or straggling learners.
en.result: Achieves strictly zero global downtime in simulated environments with millions of chips, significantly improves training efficiency, and maintains competitive performance on text and vision tasks for both dense and mixture-of-expert architectures.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how asynchronous decoupling and fault tolerance can improve robustness and efficiency in large-scale distributed training.

idea_spark.idea: Combining chaos engineering principles with adaptive grace windows and dynamic weighted merging could be further explored for adaptive optimization in heterogeneous hardware or dynamic network environments.
idea_spark.risk: Asynchronous updates may lead to convergence instability or accuracy loss, requiring careful tuning of quorum and merging strategies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Drawing inspiration from chaos engineering to proactively inject faults and design fault-tolerant mechanisms inspires similar approaches to improve reliability in more AI systems.

papers.title: DualSplat: Robust 3D Gaussian Splatting via Pseudo-Mask Bootstrapping from Reconstruction Failures
papers.summary: While 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) achieves real-time photorealistic rendering, its performance degrades significantly when training images contain transient objects that violate multi-view consistency. Existing methods face a circular dependency: accurate transient detection requires a well-reconstructed static scene, while clean reconstruction itself depends on reliable transient masks. We address this challenge with DualSplat, a Failure-to-Prior framework that converts first-pass reconstruction failures into explicit priors for a second reconstruction stage. We observe that transients, which appear in only a subset of views, often manifest as incomplete fragments during conservative initial training. We exploit these failures to construct object-level pseudo-masks by combining photometric residuals, feature mismatches, and SAM2 instance boundaries. These pseudo-masks then guide a clean second-pass 3DGS optimization, while a lightweight MLP refines them online by gradually shifting from prior supervision to self-consistency. Experiments on RobustNeRF and NeRF On-the-go show that DualSplat outperforms existing baselines, demonstrating particularly clear advantages in transient-heavy scenes and transient regions.

ai.tldr: 提出DualSplat，通过将首次重建失败转化为伪掩码先验，实现鲁棒的3D高斯泼溅，有效处理瞬态物体干扰。
ai.motivation: 现有3DGS在训练图像包含瞬态物体时性能严重下降，且瞬态检测与干净重建存在循环依赖。
ai.method: 采用Failure-to-Prior框架：第一阶段保守训练产生不完整碎片，结合光度残差、特征不匹配和SAM2实例边界构建物体级伪掩码；第二阶段用伪掩码引导干净优化，轻量MLP在线精化掩码。
ai.result: 在RobustNeRF和NeRF On-the-go数据集上超越现有基线，尤其在瞬态密集场景和瞬态区域优势明显。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需人工标注即可自动去除瞬态物体的3D重建方法，适用于动态场景的鲁棒渲染。

idea_spark.idea: 利用SAM2的实例分割能力与重建失败信号结合，自动生成高质量伪标签，打破循环依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 伪掩码质量高度依赖SAM2的边界精度，在极端遮挡或纹理匮乏区域可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将重建失败视为有用信号而非噪声，启发其他任务中利用失败案例进行自监督学习。

zh.tldr: 提出DualSplat，通过将首次重建失败转化为伪掩码先验，实现鲁棒的3D高斯泼溅，有效处理瞬态物体干扰。
zh.motivation: 现有3DGS在训练图像包含瞬态物体时性能严重下降，且瞬态检测与干净重建存在循环依赖。
zh.method: 采用Failure-to-Prior框架：第一阶段保守训练产生不完整碎片，结合光度残差、特征不匹配和SAM2实例边界构建物体级伪掩码；第二阶段用伪掩码引导干净优化，轻量MLP在线精化掩码。
zh.result: 在RobustNeRF和NeRF On-the-go数据集上超越现有基线，尤其在瞬态密集场景和瞬态区域优势明显。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种无需人工标注即可自动去除瞬态物体的3D重建方法，适用于动态场景的鲁棒渲染。

idea_spark.idea: 利用SAM2的实例分割能力与重建失败信号结合，自动生成高质量伪标签，打破循环依赖。
idea_spark.risk: 伪掩码质量高度依赖SAM2的边界精度，在极端遮挡或纹理匮乏区域可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将重建失败视为有用信号而非噪声，启发其他任务中利用失败案例进行自监督学习。

en.tldr: Proposes DualSplat, which converts first-pass reconstruction failures into pseudo-mask priors for robust 3D Gaussian Splatting, effectively handling transient objects.
en.motivation: Existing 3DGS degrades significantly when training images contain transient objects, and transient detection vs. clean reconstruction suffers from a circular dependency.
en.method: Uses a Failure-to-Prior framework: conservative initial training yields incomplete fragments; combines photometric residuals, feature mismatches, and SAM2 instance boundaries to build object-level pseudo-masks; second-stage optimization guided by masks, with a lightweight MLP refining masks online.
en.result: Outperforms baselines on RobustNeRF and NeRF On-the-go, especially in transient-heavy scenes and transient regions.
en.help_to_user: Provides an automatic transient-object removal method for 3D reconstruction without manual annotation, enabling robust rendering in dynamic scenes.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage SAM2 instance segmentation combined with reconstruction failure signals to automatically generate high-quality pseudo-labels, breaking the circular dependency.
idea_spark.risk: Pseudo-mask quality heavily depends on SAM2 boundary accuracy, which may introduce noise in extreme occlusion or textureless regions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Treating reconstruction failures as useful signals rather than noise inspires self-supervised learning from failures in other tasks.

papers.title: EDU-Net: Retinal Pathological Fluid Segmentation in OCT Images with Multiscale Feature Fusion and Boundary Optimization
papers.summary: Objective: Diabetic macular edema (DME) is the leading cause of severe visual impairment in patients with diabetes. Quantification of retinal fluid, particularly intraretinal fluid (IRF) and subretinal fluid (SRF), plays a critical role in the management of DME. Although optical coherence tomography (OCT) can be used for detection, the variable morphology of fluid accumulation and the blurred boundaries caused by noise interference still limit the accuracy of OCT's automatic segmentation. Methods: Retrospective model development and validation study. This study proposes a novel edge-guided dual-branch encoder-decoder network (EDU-Net) to achieve accurate and efficient automatic segmentation of OCT liquid lesions. The local feature extraction branch is based on the EfficientNet model, which precisely captures tiny lesions by leveraging its lightweight separable convolution and high-resolution feature preservation strategy. The global feature extraction branch is based on the large-kernel efficient convolution (LKEC) module and the downsampling layer design to enhance long-range dependencies and global semantics. EDU-Net applies a multi-category edge-guided attention module to fuse high-frequency boundary detail information to each resolution feature to optimize the boundary segmentation performance. Results: Extensive results on the in-house and public datasets demonstrate that EDU-Net achieves state-of-the-art DSC segmentation performance in terms of efficiency and robustness, especially in the segmentation of IRF lesions. Conclusions: EDU-Net integrates local details with global context and optimizes boundaries, achieving an improvement in the accuracy of automatic segmentation of retinal fluid.

ai.tldr: 提出EDU-Net网络，通过双分支编码器-解码器结构融合局部细节与全局上下文，并利用边缘引导注意力模块优化边界，实现OCT图像中视网膜病理性液体的高精度自动分割。
ai.motivation: 糖尿病黄斑水肿患者视网膜液体积聚形态多变，且噪声干扰导致边界模糊，现有自动分割方法精度受限。
ai.method: 设计局部特征提取分支（基于EfficientNet）和全局特征提取分支（基于大核高效卷积模块），并通过多类别边缘引导注意力模块将高频边界信息融合到各分辨率特征中。
ai.result: 在内部和公开数据集上，EDU-Net在DSC分割性能上达到最优，尤其在IRF病变分割中表现突出。
ai.help_to_user: 可辅助医生自动量化OCT图像中的视网膜积液，提高DME诊断与治疗监测的效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部细节与全局上下文双分支融合策略应用于其他模态（如MRI）的病变分割。
idea_spark.risk: 大核卷积可能增加计算开销，需在效率与精度间权衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用高频边界信息优化分割边界的方法可启发其他模糊边界分割问题的解决。

zh.tldr: 提出EDU-Net网络，通过双分支编码器-解码器结构融合局部细节与全局上下文，并利用边缘引导注意力模块优化边界，实现OCT图像中视网膜病理性液体的高精度自动分割。
zh.motivation: 糖尿病黄斑水肿患者视网膜液体积聚形态多变，且噪声干扰导致边界模糊，现有自动分割方法精度受限。
zh.method: 设计局部特征提取分支（基于EfficientNet）和全局特征提取分支（基于大核高效卷积模块），并通过多类别边缘引导注意力模块将高频边界信息融合到各分辨率特征中。
zh.result: 在内部和公开数据集上，EDU-Net在DSC分割性能上达到最优，尤其在IRF病变分割中表现突出。
zh.help_to_user: 可辅助医生自动量化OCT图像中的视网膜积液，提高DME诊断与治疗监测的效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将局部细节与全局上下文双分支融合策略应用于其他模态（如MRI）的病变分割。
idea_spark.risk: 大核卷积可能增加计算开销，需在效率与精度间权衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用高频边界信息优化分割边界的方法可启发其他模糊边界分割问题的解决。

en.tldr: Proposes EDU-Net, a dual-branch encoder-decoder network that fuses local details with global context and optimizes boundaries via an edge-guided attention module, achieving high-precision automatic segmentation of retinal pathological fluid in OCT images.
en.motivation: Variable morphology of fluid accumulation and blurred boundaries due to noise limit the accuracy of existing automatic segmentation methods for DME in OCT.
en.method: Designs a local feature extraction branch (based on EfficientNet) and a global feature extraction branch (based on large-kernel efficient convolution modules), and fuses high-frequency boundary information into each resolution feature via a multi-category edge-guided attention module.
en.result: On in-house and public datasets, EDU-Net achieves state-of-the-art DSC segmentation performance, especially for IRF lesion segmentation.
en.help_to_user: Assists clinicians in automatically quantifying retinal fluid in OCT images, improving efficiency in DME diagnosis and treatment monitoring.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the dual-branch fusion strategy of local details and global context to lesion segmentation in other modalities (e.g., MRI).
idea_spark.risk: Large-kernel convolutions may increase computational cost, requiring a trade-off between efficiency and accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using high-frequency boundary information to optimize segmentation boundaries can inspire solutions for other blurry-boundary segmentation problems.

papers.title: From Research Question to Scientific Workflow: Leveraging Agentic AI for Science Automation
papers.summary: Scientific workflow systems automate execution -- scheduling, fault tolerance, resource management -- but not the semantic translation that precedes it. Scientists still manually convert research questions into workflow specifications, a task requiring both domain knowledge and infrastructure expertise. We propose an agentic architecture that closes this gap through three layers: an LLM interprets natural language into structured intents (semantic layer); validated generators produce reproducible workflow DAGs (deterministic layer); and domain experts author ``Skills'': markdown documents encoding vocabulary mappings, parameter constraints, and optimization strategies (knowledge layer). This decomposition confines LLM non-determinism to intent extraction: identical intents always yield identical workflows. We implement and evaluate the architecture on the 1000 Genomes population genetics workflow and Hyperflow WMS running on Kubernetes. In an ablation study on 150 queries, Skills raise full-match intent accuracy from 44% to 83%; skill-driven deferred workflow generation reduces data transfer by 92\%; and the end-to-end pipeline completes queries on Kubernetes with LLM overhead below 15 seconds and cost under $0.001 per query.

ai.tldr: 提出一种三层智能体架构，将研究问题自动转化为可复现的科学工作流，在1000 Genomes工作流上实现83%的意图准确率和92%的数据传输减少。
ai.motivation: 科学家手动将研究问题转化为工作流规范，既需领域知识又需基础设施专长，效率低下且易出错。
ai.method: 三层架构：语义层用LLM将自然语言转为结构化意图；确定性层用验证生成器产生可复现工作流DAG；知识层由领域专家编写Skills文档编码词汇映射、参数约束和优化策略。
ai.result: 150个查询消融实验：Skills将完全匹配意图准确率从44%提升至83%；延迟工作流生成减少92%数据传输；端到端管道在Kubernetes上LLM开销低于15秒，每次查询成本低于0.001美元。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助科学家无需掌握工作流系统细节即可自动生成可复现的科学计算流程。

idea_spark.idea: 将Skills知识层扩展为社区可贡献的开放知识库，支持跨领域工作流复用。
idea_spark.risk: LLM非确定性可能引入意图误解，需严格验证机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合LLM的语义理解与确定性生成，实现科学工作流的端到端自动化。

zh.tldr: 提出一种三层智能体架构，将研究问题自动转化为可复现的科学工作流，在1000 Genomes工作流上实现83%的意图准确率和92%的数据传输减少。
zh.motivation: 科学家手动将研究问题转化为工作流规范，既需领域知识又需基础设施专长，效率低下且易出错。

zh.result: 150个查询消融实验：Skills将完全匹配意图准确率从44%提升至83%；延迟工作流生成减少92%数据传输；端到端管道在Kubernetes上LLM开销低于15秒，每次查询成本低于0.001美元。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助科学家无需掌握工作流系统细节即可自动生成可复现的科学计算流程。

idea_spark.idea: 将Skills知识层扩展为社区可贡献的开放知识库，支持跨领域工作流复用。
idea_spark.risk: LLM非确定性可能引入意图误解，需严格验证机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合LLM的语义理解与确定性生成，实现科学工作流的端到端自动化。

en.tldr: Proposes a three-layer agentic architecture that automatically translates research questions into reproducible scientific workflows, achieving 83% intent accuracy and 92% data transfer reduction on the 1000 Genomes workflow.
en.motivation: Scientists manually convert research questions into workflow specifications, requiring both domain knowledge and infrastructure expertise, which is inefficient and error-prone.
en.method: Three-layer architecture: semantic layer uses LLM to interpret natural language into structured intents; deterministic layer uses validated generators to produce reproducible workflow DAGs; knowledge layer has domain experts author Skills documents encoding vocabulary mappings, parameter constraints, and optimization strategies.
en.result: Ablation study on 150 queries: Skills raise full-match intent accuracy from 44% to 83%; deferred workflow generation reduces data transfer by 92%; end-to-end pipeline on Kubernetes has LLM overhead below 15 seconds and cost under $0.001 per query.
en.help_to_user: Helps scientists automatically generate reproducible scientific workflows without needing to master workflow system details.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the Skills knowledge layer into a community-contributed open knowledge base to support cross-domain workflow reuse.
idea_spark.risk: LLM non-determinism may introduce intent misinterpretation, requiring rigorous validation mechanisms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine LLM semantic understanding with deterministic generation for end-to-end scientific workflow automation.

papers.title: Co-Evolving LLM Decision and Skill Bank Agents for Long-Horizon Tasks
papers.summary: Long horizon interactive environments are a testbed for evaluating agents skill usage abilities. These environments demand multi step reasoning, the chaining of multiple skills over many timesteps, and robust decision making under delayed rewards and partial observability. Games are a good testbed for evaluating agent skill usage in environments. Large Language Models (LLMs) offer a promising alternative as game playing agents, but they often struggle with consistent long horizon decision making because they lack a mechanism to discover, retain, and reuse structured skills across episodes. We present COSPLAY, a co evolution framework in which an LLM decision agent retrieves skills from a learnable skill bank to guide action taking, while an agent managed skill pipeline discovers reusable skills from the agents unlabeled rollouts to form a skill bank. Our framework improves both the decision agent to learn better skill retrieval and action generation, while the skill bank agent continually extracts, refines, and updates skills together with their contracts. Experiments across six game environments show that COSPLAY with an 8B base model achieves over 25.1 percent average reward improvement against four frontier LLM baselines on single player game benchmarks while remaining competitive on multi player social reasoning games.

ai.tldr: 提出COSPLAY框架，通过LLM决策智能体与可学习技能库的协同进化，提升长时域任务中的技能检索与动作生成能力。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM在长时域交互环境中缺乏发现、保留和复用结构化技能的机制，导致决策不一致。
ai.method: COSPLAY框架包含一个LLM决策智能体（从技能库检索技能指导动作）和一个技能管线智能体（从无标签轨迹中提取、精炼并更新技能）。
ai.result: 在六个游戏环境中，使用8B基础模型的COSPLAY在单人游戏基准上平均奖励提升超过25.1%，在多人社交推理游戏中保持竞争力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过技能库与决策智能体的协同进化，提升LLM在复杂长时域任务中的表现。

idea_spark.idea: 将技能库与决策智能体解耦，通过无标签轨迹自动发现可复用技能，减少人工标注成本。
idea_spark.risk: 技能库可能包含冗余或冲突技能，影响检索效率；多智能体协同可能增加训练不稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物进化中技能传承与创新的启发，模拟智能体在环境中不断积累和优化技能的过程。

zh.tldr: 提出COSPLAY框架，通过LLM决策智能体与可学习技能库的协同进化，提升长时域任务中的技能检索与动作生成能力。
zh.motivation: 现有LLM在长时域交互环境中缺乏发现、保留和复用结构化技能的机制，导致决策不一致。
zh.method: COSPLAY框架包含一个LLM决策智能体（从技能库检索技能指导动作）和一个技能管线智能体（从无标签轨迹中提取、精炼并更新技能）。
zh.result: 在六个游戏环境中，使用8B基础模型的COSPLAY在单人游戏基准上平均奖励提升超过25.1%，在多人社交推理游戏中保持竞争力。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过技能库与决策智能体的协同进化，提升LLM在复杂长时域任务中的表现。

idea_spark.idea: 将技能库与决策智能体解耦，通过无标签轨迹自动发现可复用技能，减少人工标注成本。
idea_spark.risk: 技能库可能包含冗余或冲突技能，影响检索效率；多智能体协同可能增加训练不稳定性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受生物进化中技能传承与创新的启发，模拟智能体在环境中不断积累和优化技能的过程。

en.tldr: Proposes COSPLAY, a co-evolution framework where an LLM decision agent retrieves skills from a learnable skill bank, while a skill pipeline agent discovers reusable skills from unlabeled rollouts, improving long-horizon task performance.
en.motivation: Existing LLMs lack mechanisms to discover, retain, and reuse structured skills across episodes, leading to inconsistent long-horizon decision making.
en.method: COSPLAY consists of an LLM decision agent that retrieves skills from a skill bank to guide actions, and a skill pipeline agent that extracts, refines, and updates skills from unlabeled agent rollouts.
en.result: On six game environments, COSPLAY with an 8B base model achieves over 25.1% average reward improvement against frontier LLM baselines on single-player games and remains competitive on multi-player social reasoning games.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how co-evolving a skill bank with a decision agent can enhance LLM performance in complex long-horizon tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple the skill bank from the decision agent and automatically discover reusable skills from unlabeled trajectories, reducing manual annotation cost.
idea_spark.risk: The skill bank may contain redundant or conflicting skills, affecting retrieval efficiency; multi-agent co-evolution may increase training instability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by biological evolution where skills are inherited and innovated, simulating agents that accumulate and optimize skills through environmental interaction.

papers.title: On the Challenges of Holistic Intrusion Detection in ICS
papers.summary: Past attacks against industrial control systems (ICS) show that adversaries often target both the ICS network and the physical process to achieve potential catastrophic impact. To secure ICS, intrusion detection systems promise timely uncovering of such adversaries. However, as these detection mechanisms typically focus on isolated characteristics of ICS (e.g., packet timings), multiple detection systems have to be deployed in parallel, complicating their operation in practice. In this work, to spur discussion and further research, we present challenges encountered during our research towards a holistic intrusion detection system aiming to cover all dimensions of an ICS.

ai.tldr: 本文讨论了在工业控制系统中实现全面入侵检测所面临的挑战，指出现有检测机制通常只关注单一特征，导致需要并行部署多个系统，增加了操作复杂性。
ai.motivation: 针对工业控制系统的攻击常同时针对网络和物理过程，现有入侵检测系统仅关注孤立特征，需并行部署多个系统，操作复杂，亟需全面覆盖所有维度的检测方案。
ai.method: 通过分析现有检测机制的局限性，提出构建全面入侵检测系统所需克服的挑战，并基于研究经验进行讨论。
ai.result: 识别出实现全面入侵检测的关键挑战，包括覆盖网络和物理过程、减少系统并行部署的复杂性等。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员了解当前ICS入侵检测的不足，明确未来研究方向。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个统一框架，融合网络流量分析与物理过程异常检测，减少系统冗余。
idea_spark.risk: 统一框架可能增加单点故障风险，且物理过程建模的准确性要求高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴多模态融合技术，将网络数据与传感器数据结合，实现更全面的检测。

zh.tldr: 本文讨论了在工业控制系统中实现全面入侵检测所面临的挑战，指出现有检测机制通常只关注单一特征，导致需要并行部署多个系统，增加了操作复杂性。

zh.method: 通过分析现有检测机制的局限性，提出构建全面入侵检测系统所需克服的挑战，并基于研究经验进行讨论。
zh.result: 识别出实现全面入侵检测的关键挑战，包括覆盖网络和物理过程、减少系统并行部署的复杂性等。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员了解当前ICS入侵检测的不足，明确未来研究方向。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个统一框架，融合网络流量分析与物理过程异常检测，减少系统冗余。
idea_spark.risk: 统一框架可能增加单点故障风险，且物理过程建模的准确性要求高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴多模态融合技术，将网络数据与传感器数据结合，实现更全面的检测。

en.tldr: This paper discusses the challenges of achieving holistic intrusion detection in industrial control systems, noting that existing mechanisms focus on isolated features, requiring parallel deployment of multiple systems and increasing operational complexity.
en.motivation: Attacks on ICS often target both network and physical processes; current IDS focus on isolated characteristics, necessitating parallel deployment of multiple systems, which is operationally complex. A holistic detection system covering all dimensions is needed.
en.method: By analyzing limitations of existing detection mechanisms, the paper presents challenges encountered during research towards a holistic intrusion detection system, based on research experience.
en.result: Key challenges for holistic intrusion detection are identified, including covering network and physical processes and reducing system deployment complexity.
en.help_to_user: Helps security researchers understand current shortcomings in ICS intrusion detection and clarifies future research directions.

idea_spark.idea: Design a unified framework that integrates network traffic analysis and physical process anomaly detection to reduce system redundancy.
idea_spark.risk: A unified framework may increase single point of failure risk, and physical process modeling requires high accuracy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage multimodal fusion techniques to combine network data with sensor data for more comprehensive detection.

papers.title: UKP_Psycontrol at SemEval-2026 Task 2: Modeling Valence and Arousal Dynamics from Text
papers.summary: This paper presents our system developed for SemEval-2026 Task 2. The task requires modeling both current affect and short-term affective change in chronologically ordered user-generated texts. We explore three complementary approaches: (1) LLM prompting under user-aware and user-agnostic settings, (2) a pairwise Maximum Entropy (MaxEnt) model with Ising-style interactions for structured transition modeling, and (3) a lightweight neural regression model incorporating recent affective trajectories and trainable user embeddings. Our findings indicate that LLMs effectively capture static affective signals from text, whereas short-term affective variation in this dataset is more strongly explained by recent numeric state trajectories than by textual semantics. Our system ranked first among participating teams in both Subtask 1 and Subtask 2A based on the official evaluation metric.

ai.tldr: 本文提出了一个用于SemEval-2026任务2的系统，结合大语言模型提示、成对最大熵模型和轻量级神经回归，对用户生成文本中的效价和唤醒动态进行建模，在两个子任务中均排名第一。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以同时捕捉文本中的静态情感信号和短期情感变化，需要更有效的建模框架。
ai.method: 探索三种互补方法：大语言模型提示（考虑用户感知与无感知）、基于伊辛相互作用的成对最大熵模型、以及结合近期情感轨迹和可训练用户嵌入的轻量级神经回归。
ai.result: 大语言模型能有效捕捉静态情感信号，而短期情感变化更多由近期数值轨迹而非文本语义解释；系统在官方评估指标下获得子任务1和2A的第一名。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员理解如何结合大语言模型和结构化模型进行情感动态建模，并提供实际竞赛获胜方案。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将用户嵌入与情感轨迹作为先验知识注入大语言模型，以增强对短期变化的预测能力。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖用户嵌入可能引入隐私问题，且模型在跨领域泛化时可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受伊辛模型在物理系统中模拟相互作用的启发，将其用于文本情感状态间的结构化转换建模。

zh.tldr: 本文提出了一个用于SemEval-2026任务2的系统，结合大语言模型提示、成对最大熵模型和轻量级神经回归，对用户生成文本中的效价和唤醒动态进行建模，在两个子任务中均排名第一。

en.tldr: This paper presents a system for SemEval-2026 Task 2 that combines LLM prompting, a pairwise Maximum Entropy model, and lightweight neural regression to model valence and arousal dynamics from user-generated texts, achieving first place in both subtasks.
en.motivation: Existing methods struggle to simultaneously capture static affective signals and short-term affective changes from text, necessitating a more effective modeling framework.
en.method: Explores three complementary approaches: LLM prompting (user-aware and user-agnostic), a pairwise Maximum Entropy model with Ising-style interactions, and a lightweight neural regression incorporating recent affective trajectories and trainable user embeddings.
en.result: LLMs effectively capture static affective signals, while short-term affective variation is better explained by recent numeric state trajectories than textual semantics; the system ranked first in both Subtask 1 and Subtask 2A.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers understand how to combine LLMs and structured models for affective dynamics modeling, and provides a winning solution for a shared task.

idea_spark.idea: Explore injecting user embeddings and affective trajectories as priors into LLMs to enhance prediction of short-term changes.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on user embeddings may raise privacy concerns, and the model may fail to generalize across domains.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the Ising model for simulating interactions in physics, applied here to model structured transitions between textual affective states.

papers.title: GeoMind: An Agentic Workflow for Lithology Classification with Reasoned Tool Invocation
papers.summary: Lithology classification in well logs is a fundamental geoscience data mining task that aims to infer rock types from multi dimensional geophysical sequences. Despite recent progress, existing approaches typically formulate the problem as a static, single-step discriminative mapping. This static paradigm limits evidence-based diagnostic reasoning against geological standards, often yielding predictions that are detached from geological reality due to a lack of domain priors. In this work, we propose GeoMind, a tool-augmented agentic framework that models lithology classification as a sequential reasoning process. GeoMind organizes its toolkit into perception, reasoning, and analysis modules, which respectively translate raw logs into semantic trends, infer lithology hypotheses from multi-source evidence, and verify predictions against stratigraphic constraints. A global planner adaptively coordinates these modules based on input characteristics, enabling geologically plausible and evidence-grounded decisions. To guarantee the logical consistency of GeoMind, we introduce a fine-grained process supervision strategy. Unlike standard methods that focus solely on final outcomes, our approach optimizes intermediate reasoning steps, ensuring the validity of decision trajectories and alignment to geological constraints. Experiments on four benchmark well-log datasets demonstrate that GeoMind consistently outperforms strong baselines in classification performance while providing transparent and traceable decision-making processes.

ai.tldr: 提出GeoMind，一种工具增强的智能体框架，将岩性分类建模为顺序推理过程，通过感知、推理和分析模块实现地质合理的决策。
ai.motivation: 现有岩性分类方法采用静态单步判别映射，缺乏基于地质标准的证据推理，预测结果常脱离地质现实。
ai.method: GeoMind包含感知、推理和分析模块，分别处理原始测井数据、从多源证据推断岩性假设、验证预测是否符合地层约束，并由全局规划器自适应协调。
ai.result: 在四个基准测井数据集上，GeoMind在分类性能上持续优于强基线，并提供透明可追溯的决策过程。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用智能体工作流和过程监督提升岩性分类的准确性和可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合过程监督与多模块智能体，可推广至需要多步推理和领域约束验证的复杂科学问题。
idea_spark.risk: 过程监督可能增加训练复杂度，且对地质约束的依赖可能限制在缺乏先验知识的场景中的泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受启发于将岩性分类从静态映射转变为动态推理，类似思路可用于其他需要证据链和领域知识对齐的预测任务。

idea_spark.risk: 过程监督可能增加训练复杂度，且对地质约束的依赖可能限制在缺乏先验知识的场景中的泛化能力。

en.tldr: Proposes GeoMind, a tool-augmented agentic framework that models lithology classification as a sequential reasoning process with perception, reasoning, and analysis modules for geologically plausible decisions.
en.motivation: Existing lithology classification methods use static single-step discriminative mapping, lacking evidence-based reasoning against geological standards, leading to predictions detached from geological reality.
en.method: GeoMind includes perception, reasoning, and analysis modules to process raw logs, infer lithology hypotheses from multi-source evidence, and verify predictions against stratigraphic constraints, coordinated by a global planner.
en.result: On four benchmark well-log datasets, GeoMind consistently outperforms strong baselines in classification performance while providing transparent and traceable decision-making.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how agentic workflows and process supervision can improve accuracy and interpretability in lithology classification.

idea_spark.idea: Combining process supervision with multi-module agents can be extended to complex scientific problems requiring multi-step reasoning and domain constraint verification.
idea_spark.risk: Process supervision may increase training complexity, and reliance on geological constraints may limit generalization in scenarios lacking prior knowledge.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by transforming lithology classification from static mapping to dynamic reasoning; similar approaches could benefit other prediction tasks requiring evidence chains and domain alignment.

papers.title: Hierarchical Joint Source-Channel Coding with Constrained Information Leakage
papers.summary: This paper studies the hierarchical joint source-channel coding with information leakage constraint in the first-phase reconstruction and distortion constraints. The receiver's access to the data varies and is evaluated by the quality of the side information. Due to the consideration of channel capacity limitation or the efficiency of the system performance, the encoder may send some additional information in Phase 1 that can only be decoded in Phase 2 with higher-quality side information. While this can optimize the overall performance, the additional information causes excessive information leakage. We provide general inner and outer bounds for the conditions such that a given distortion-leakage pair $(D_1,D_2,L)$ is achievable, together with a capacity-achieving condition.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了具有信息泄漏约束的分层联合源信道编码，给出了可达失真-泄漏对的内外边界及容量可达条件。
ai.motivation: 在分层通信中，第一阶段发送的额外信息虽能优化整体性能，但会导致信息过度泄漏，需在失真与泄漏间取得平衡。
ai.method: 通过建立一般的内外边界，分析给定失真-泄漏对的可达性，并给出容量可达条件。
ai.result: 得到了分层联合源信道编码下失真-泄漏对可达的充分必要条件及容量可达条件。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解分层编码中信息泄漏与系统性能的权衡，为设计安全高效的通信系统提供理论指导。

idea_spark.idea: 考虑将信息泄漏约束与自适应编码结合，根据信道状态动态调整第一阶段发送的额外信息量。
idea_spark.risk: 实际系统中信道状态估计误差可能导致泄漏约束难以精确满足，需鲁棒性设计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受分层编码中信息泄漏与性能权衡的启发，可探索在联邦学习中控制梯度泄漏的类似机制。

en.tldr: This paper studies hierarchical joint source-channel coding with information leakage constraints, providing inner and outer bounds for achievable distortion-leakage pairs and a capacity-achieving condition.
en.motivation: In hierarchical communication, additional information sent in Phase 1 can optimize overall performance but causes excessive information leakage, requiring a trade-off between distortion and leakage.
en.method: General inner and outer bounds are derived to characterize the achievability of given distortion-leakage pairs, along with a capacity-achieving condition.
en.result: Necessary and sufficient conditions for achievable distortion-leakage pairs and capacity-achieving conditions are obtained.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the trade-off between information leakage and system performance in hierarchical coding, providing theoretical guidance for designing secure and efficient communication systems.

idea_spark.idea: Consider combining information leakage constraints with adaptive coding to dynamically adjust the amount of additional information sent in Phase 1 based on channel conditions.
idea_spark.risk: Channel state estimation errors in practical systems may make it difficult to precisely satisfy leakage constraints, requiring robust design.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the trade-off between information leakage and performance in hierarchical coding, similar mechanisms could be explored to control gradient leakage in federated learning.

papers.title: LLM-Steered Power Allocation for Parallel QPSK-AWGN Channels
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly being explored as high-level decision modules in closed-loop systems, but their stochastic nature makes safe integration challenging. In this paper, we propose LLM-Steered Power Allocation, a dual-process architecture for parallel QPSK channels inspired by Kahneman's System 1/System 2 framework. A fast numerical optimizer (System 1) continuously performs projected gradient ascent on a weighted mutual-information objective, while an LLM navigator (System 2) periodically interprets natural-language policies and updates only the channel weights and the operational power budget. The LLM never manipulates the power-allocation variables directly, and constraint satisfaction is enforced structurally by the optimizer. To mitigate LLM unreliability, we further incorporate multi-layer guardrails including normalization, exponential moving-average smoothing, and fallback mechanisms. Numerical experiments on an 8-channel system show that, with a fixed optimization core and unchanged system prompt, different natural-language policies induce qualitatively different operating points, including throughput-oriented allocation, channel prioritization, power-aware operation, and channel shutdown. In addition, under an abrupt channel-gain reversal, the proposed system autonomously reconfigures its steering signals and reduces the final mutual-information spread by 60% compared with the optimizer alone. These results suggest that LLMs can serve as policy interpreters for safe, flexible reconfiguration of communication-system optimizers without controller reimplementation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种LLM引导的并行QPSK-AWGN信道功率分配架构，结合快速数值优化器（系统1）和LLM导航器（系统2），通过多层防护机制确保安全，实验表明LLM可安全解释自然语言策略并灵活重配置通信优化器。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型在闭环系统中作为高层决策模块具有潜力，但其随机性带来安全挑战，需要一种结构化的方法确保可靠集成。
ai.method: 采用双过程架构：系统1执行投影梯度上升优化加权互信息目标；系统2（LLM）仅更新信道权重和功率预算，不直接操作功率变量，并引入归一化、指数移动平均平滑和回退机制等多层防护。
ai.result: 在8信道系统中，不同自然语言策略产生不同工作点（如吞吐量导向、信道优先级、功率感知、信道关闭）；在信道增益突变下，系统自主重配置，互信息扩散减少60%。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了如何安全地将LLM集成到通信系统优化中，为需要灵活策略解释和重配置的应用提供参考。

idea_spark.idea: 将LLM作为策略解释器，结合传统优化器，实现安全、灵活的系统重配置，无需重新实现控制器。
idea_spark.risk: LLM的随机输出可能导致策略不一致，需依赖防护机制；LLM推理延迟可能影响实时性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受Kahneman系统1/系统2框架启发，将快速直觉（优化器）与慢速推理（LLM）结合，为AI安全集成提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes an LLM-steered power allocation architecture for parallel QPSK-AWGN channels, combining a fast numerical optimizer (System 1) and an LLM navigator (System 2) with multi-layer guardrails for safe integration. Experiments show LLMs can interpret natural-language policies and flexibly reconfigure the optimizer without controller reimplementation.
en.motivation: Large language models show promise as high-level decision modules in closed-loop systems, but their stochastic nature poses safety challenges, requiring a structured approach for reliable integration.
en.method: Dual-process architecture: System 1 performs projected gradient ascent on a weighted mutual-information objective; System 2 (LLM) updates only channel weights and power budget, never directly manipulating power variables, with guardrails including normalization, exponential moving-average smoothing, and fallback mechanisms.
en.result: On an 8-channel system, different natural-language policies yield distinct operating points (e.g., throughput-oriented, channel prioritization, power-aware, channel shutdown); under abrupt channel-gain reversal, the system autonomously reconfigures, reducing mutual-information spread by 60%.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates safe integration of LLMs into communication system optimization, providing a reference for applications requiring flexible policy interpretation and reconfiguration.

idea_spark.idea: Use LLMs as policy interpreters combined with traditional optimizers for safe, flexible system reconfiguration without controller reimplementation.
idea_spark.risk: Stochastic LLM outputs may cause policy inconsistency, requiring guardrails; LLM inference latency may impact real-time performance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Kahneman's System 1/System 2 framework, combining fast intuition (optimizer) with slow reasoning (LLM) offers a new approach for safe AI integration.

papers.title: Efficient Logic Gate Networks for Video Copy Detection
papers.summary: Video copy detection requires robust similarity estimation under diverse visual distortions while operating at very large scale. Although deep neural networks achieve strong performance, their computational cost and descriptor size limit practical deployment in high-throughput systems. In this work, we propose a video copy detection framework based on differentiable Logic Gate Networks (LGNs), which replace conventional floating-point feature extractors with compact, logic-based representations. Our approach combines aggressive frame miniaturization, binary preprocessing, and a trainable LGN embedding model that learns both logical operations and interconnections. After training, the model can be discretized into a purely Boolean circuit, enabling extremely fast and memory-efficient inference. We systematically evaluate different similarity strategies, binarization schemes, and LGN architectures across multiple dataset folds and difficulty levels. Experimental results demonstrate that LGN-based models achieve competitive or superior accuracy and ranking performance compared to prior models, while producing descriptors several orders of magnitude smaller and delivering inference speeds exceeding 11k samples per second. These findings indicate that logic-based models offer a promising alternative for scalable and resource-efficient video copy detection.

ai.tldr: 提出基于可微逻辑门网络（LGN）的视频拷贝检测框架，用紧凑的布尔电路替代浮点特征提取器，实现高速、低内存的推理。
ai.motivation: 深度神经网络在视频拷贝检测中性能好但计算开销大、描述符尺寸大，难以部署于大规模高吞吐系统。
ai.method: 结合极端帧缩小、二值预处理和可训练LGN嵌入模型，训练后离散化为纯布尔电路，并系统评估不同相似度策略、二值化方案和LGN架构。
ai.result: LGN模型在准确率和排序性能上达到或超越先前模型，描述符尺寸小几个数量级，推理速度超过11k样本/秒。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何用逻辑电路实现高效视频拷贝检测，适合资源受限或高吞吐场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将LGN与哈希学习结合，直接输出二值哈希码，进一步简化存储和匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 极端二值化可能丢失细粒度信息，对复杂变形（如严重遮挡）的鲁棒性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受FPGA中逻辑电路高效性的启发，将神经网络推理转化为硬件友好的布尔运算。

idea_spark.idea: 将LGN与哈希学习结合，直接输出二值哈希码，进一步简化存储和匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 极端二值化可能丢失细粒度信息，对复杂变形（如严重遮挡）的鲁棒性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受FPGA中逻辑电路高效性的启发，将神经网络推理转化为硬件友好的布尔运算。

en.tldr: Proposes a video copy detection framework using differentiable Logic Gate Networks (LGNs), replacing floating-point feature extractors with compact Boolean circuits for fast and memory-efficient inference.
en.motivation: Deep neural networks achieve strong performance in video copy detection but suffer from high computational cost and large descriptor size, hindering deployment in large-scale high-throughput systems.
en.method: Combines aggressive frame miniaturization, binary preprocessing, and a trainable LGN embedding model; after training, the model is discretized into a purely Boolean circuit. Systematically evaluates similarity strategies, binarization schemes, and LGN architectures.
en.result: LGN-based models achieve competitive or superior accuracy and ranking performance, with descriptors orders of magnitude smaller and inference speeds exceeding 11k samples per second.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how logic circuits can enable efficient video copy detection, suitable for resource-constrained or high-throughput scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Combine LGNs with hash learning to directly output binary hash codes, further simplifying storage and matching.
idea_spark.risk: Extreme binarization may lose fine-grained information; robustness to complex distortions (e.g., severe occlusion) needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the efficiency of logic circuits in FPGAs, transforming neural network inference into hardware-friendly Boolean operations.

papers.title: DiP-SD: Distributed Pipelined Speculative Decoding for Efficient LLM Inference at the Edge
papers.summary: Speculative decoding has emerged as a promising technique for large language model (LLM) inference by accelerating autoregressive decoding via draft-then-verify. This paper studies a new edge scenario with multi-user inference, where draft tokens are generated locally on devices and subsequently offloaded to a centralized edge server for batch verification. The key challenge is to sustain high throughput under coupled decisions of (i) batching and pipeline scheduling and (ii) per user draft token length. We propose DiP-SD, which exploits two complementary parallelism dimensions: device-level distributed drafting and phase-level draft-verify pipelining. We formulate a throughput-maximization objective, defined as the expected number of accepted tokens per unit time, and jointly optimize the number of batches, user-to-batch assignment, and integer draft lengths. To solve the resulting fractional mixed-integer program, DiP-SD scans the batch number and iteratively alternates between an association subproblem and a draft-length subproblem. Numerical results under a Qwen3-1.7B/Qwen3-32B device-edge deployment show that DiP-SD achieves up to 17.89x throughput over autoregressive decoding (AD) and 1.93x over AD with greedy batching.

ai.tldr: 提出DiP-SD，一种面向边缘多用户场景的分布式流水线投机解码方案，通过设备级分布式起草和阶段级流水线验证联合优化批处理与草稿长度，显著提升LLM推理吞吐量。
ai.motivation: 现有投机解码主要针对单用户场景，而边缘多用户推理面临批处理调度与草稿长度耦合决策的挑战，导致吞吐量受限。
ai.method: 将吞吐量最大化建模为分数混合整数规划，通过扫描批次数并交替求解用户关联子问题和草稿长度子问题进行联合优化。
ai.result: 在Qwen3-1.7B/Qwen3-32B设备-边缘部署下，相比自回归解码吞吐量提升最高17.89倍，相比贪心批处理自回归解码提升1.93倍。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过分布式起草和流水线验证在边缘多用户场景下高效加速LLM推理。

idea_spark.idea: 将设备端分布式起草与边缘端批量验证流水线化，并联合优化批处理大小和草稿长度。
idea_spark.risk: 实际部署中设备异构性和网络延迟可能影响分布式起草的同步效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴了分布式计算中的流水线并行思想，将投机解码的起草和验证阶段解耦为可并行执行的子任务。

en.tldr: Proposes DiP-SD, a distributed pipelined speculative decoding scheme for multi-user edge inference, jointly optimizing batching and draft lengths via device-level distributed drafting and phase-level pipelining to significantly boost LLM throughput.
en.motivation: Existing speculative decoding focuses on single-user scenarios, while multi-user edge inference faces coupled decisions of batching and draft length, limiting throughput.
en.method: Formulates throughput maximization as a fractional mixed-integer program, solved by scanning batch count and alternating between user-assignment and draft-length subproblems.
en.result: On Qwen3-1.7B/Qwen3-32B device-edge deployment, achieves up to 17.89x throughput over autoregressive decoding and 1.93x over greedy-batched autoregressive decoding.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to accelerate LLM inference in multi-user edge scenarios via distributed drafting and pipelined verification.

idea_spark.idea: Pipeline device-level distributed drafting with edge-side batch verification, jointly optimizing batch size and draft length.
idea_spark.risk: Device heterogeneity and network latency in real deployments may affect synchronization efficiency of distributed drafting.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from pipeline parallelism in distributed computing, decoupling drafting and verification into parallelizable sub-tasks.

papers.title: Learning to Communicate: Toward End-to-End Optimization of Multi-Agent Language Systems
papers.summary: Multi-agent systems built on large language models have shown strong performance on complex reasoning tasks, yet most work focuses on agent roles and orchestration while treating inter-agent communication as a fixed interface. Latent communication through internal representations such as key-value caches offers a promising alternative to text-based protocols, but existing approaches do not jointly optimize communication with multi-agent reasoning. Therefore we propose DiffMAS, a training framework that treats latent communication as a learnable component of multi-agent systems. DiffMAS performs parameter-efficient supervised training over multi-agent latent trajectories, enabling agents to jointly learn how information should be encoded and interpreted across interactions. Experiments on mathematical reasoning, scientific QA, code generation, and commonsense benchmarks show that DiffMAS consistently improves reasoning accuracy and decoding stability over single-agent inference, text-based multi-agent systems, and prior latent communication methods, achieving 26.7% on AIME24, 20.2% on GPQA-Diamond, and consistent gains across reasoning benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 提出DiffMAS框架，将多智能体系统中的潜在通信作为可学习组件，通过参数高效监督训练联合优化通信与推理。
ai.motivation: 现有方法将智能体间通信视为固定接口，未与多智能体推理联合优化。
ai.method: DiffMAS对多智能体潜在轨迹进行参数高效监督训练，使智能体共同学习信息的编码与解读。
ai.result: 在数学推理、科学问答、代码生成和常识基准上持续提升推理准确率和解码稳定性，AIME24达26.7%，GPQA-Diamond达20.2%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种端到端优化多智能体语言系统通信的方法，提升复杂推理任务性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将潜在通信与强化学习结合，实现更灵活的通信策略学习。
idea_spark.risk: 潜在通信的可解释性不足，可能影响调试和安全性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类团队协作中沟通策略优化的启发，将通信视为可学习过程。

en.tldr: Proposes DiffMAS, a training framework that treats latent communication as a learnable component in multi-agent systems, jointly optimizing communication and reasoning via parameter-efficient supervised training.
en.motivation: Existing methods treat inter-agent communication as a fixed interface, not jointly optimized with multi-agent reasoning.
en.method: DiffMAS performs parameter-efficient supervised training over multi-agent latent trajectories, enabling agents to jointly learn encoding and interpretation of information.
en.result: Consistently improves reasoning accuracy and decoding stability on math reasoning, scientific QA, code generation, and commonsense benchmarks, achieving 26.7% on AIME24 and 20.2% on GPQA-Diamond.
en.help_to_user: Provides an end-to-end optimization method for communication in multi-agent language systems, enhancing performance on complex reasoning tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Combine latent communication with reinforcement learning for more flexible communication strategy learning.
idea_spark.risk: Latent communication may lack interpretability, affecting debugging and safety.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how human teams optimize communication strategies during collaboration.

papers.title: EdgeFormer: local patch-based edge detection transformer on point clouds
papers.summary: Edge points on 3D point clouds can clearly convey 3D geometry and surface characteristics, therefore, edge detection is widely used in many vision applications with high industrial and commercial demands. However, the fine-grained edge features are difficult to detect effectively as they are generally densely distributed or exhibit small-scale surface gradients. To address this issue, we present a learning-based edge detection network, named EdgeFormer, which mainly consists of two stages. Based on the observation that spatially neighboring points tend to exhibit high correlation, forming the local underlying surface, we convert the edge detection of the entire point cloud into a point classification based on local patches. Therefore, in the first stage, we construct local patch feature descriptors that describe the local neighborhood around each point. In the second stage, we classify each point by analyzing the local patch feature descriptors generated in the first stage. Due to the conversion of the point cloud into local patches, the proposed method can effectively extract the finer details. The experimental results show that our model demonstrates competitive performance compared to six baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出EdgeFormer，一种基于局部面片和Transformer的点云边缘检测网络，将全局边缘检测转化为局部面片分类，有效提取细粒度边缘特征。
ai.motivation: 点云边缘点能清晰传达3D几何与表面特性，但细粒度边缘特征因密集分布或小尺度梯度而难以检测。
ai.method: 两阶段网络：第一阶段为每个点构建局部面片特征描述子；第二阶段基于这些描述子对每个点进行分类。
ai.result: 在六个基线方法上表现出竞争性性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户从点云中准确检测边缘，用于3D重建、物体识别等视觉应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将点云边缘检测转化为局部面片分类，利用Transformer捕捉面片内点间的空间关系。
idea_spark.risk: 局部面片大小和采样策略可能影响泛化性，需针对不同密度点云调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受空间邻近点高相关性启发，将全局问题局部化，简化学习难度。

en.tldr: Proposes EdgeFormer, a local patch-based edge detection transformer for point clouds, converting global edge detection into local patch classification to effectively extract fine-grained edge features.
en.motivation: Edge points on 3D point clouds convey geometry and surface characteristics, but fine-grained edges are hard to detect due to dense distribution or small-scale gradients.
en.method: Two-stage network: first constructs local patch feature descriptors for each point; then classifies each point based on these descriptors.
en.result: Achieves competitive performance against six baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users accurately detect edges in point clouds for applications like 3D reconstruction and object recognition.

idea_spark.idea: Convert point cloud edge detection into local patch classification, using a transformer to capture spatial relationships among points within a patch.
idea_spark.risk: Patch size and sampling strategy may affect generalization and require tuning for different point cloud densities.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the high correlation of spatially neighboring points forming local surfaces, simplifying the learning by localizing the global problem.

papers.title: Efficient Agent Evaluation via Diversity-Guided User Simulation
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as customer-facing agents, yet evaluating their reliability remains challenging due to stochastic, multi-turn interactions. Current evaluation protocols rely on linear Monte Carlo rollouts of complete agent-user conversations to estimate success. However, this approach is computationally inefficient, repeatedly regenerating identical early prefixes, and often fails to uncover deep failure modes that arise from rare user behaviors.   We introduce DIVERT (Diversity-Induced Evaluation via Branching of Trajectories), an efficient, snapshot-based, coverage-guided user simulation framework for systematic exploration of agent-user interactions. DIVERT captures the full agent-environment state at critical decision points and resumes execution from these snapshots, enabling reuse of shared conversation prefixes and reducing redundant computation. From each junction, the framework branches using targeted, diversity-inducing user responses, allowing directed exploration of alternative interaction paths.   By focusing evaluation on semantically diverse and underexplored trajectories, DIVERT improves both efficiency and coverage. Empirical results show that it discovers more failures per token compared to standard linear rollout protocols, while expanding the set of tasks on which failures are identified.

ai.tldr: 提出DIVERT框架，通过快照分支和多样性引导的用户模拟，高效评估LLM代理的可靠性。
ai.motivation: 现有线性蒙特卡洛评估方法计算效率低，重复生成相同前缀，且难以发现罕见用户行为导致的深层故障。
ai.method: 在关键决策点捕获完整环境状态并创建快照，从快照恢复执行以复用共享对话前缀；在每个分支点使用多样性引导的用户响应，探索替代交互路径。
ai.result: 相比标准线性回放协议，DIVERT在每token上发现更多故障，并扩展了识别故障的任务集合。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和工程师更高效、更全面地评估LLM代理在复杂多轮对话中的表现。

idea_spark.idea: 结合主动学习，自动识别最可能暴露故障的决策点进行分支。
idea_spark.risk: 快照存储和恢复可能引入额外开销，需平衡粒度与效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件测试中的符号执行和路径覆盖获得启发，将类似思想应用于对话系统评估。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从软件测试中的符号执行和路径覆盖获得启发，将类似思想应用于对话系统评估。

en.tldr: Proposes DIVERT, a snapshot-based, diversity-guided user simulation framework for efficient and thorough evaluation of LLM agents.
en.motivation: Standard linear Monte Carlo rollouts are computationally wasteful, repeatedly regenerating identical prefixes, and fail to uncover deep failures from rare user behaviors.
en.method: Captures full agent-environment state at critical decision points, resumes from snapshots to reuse prefixes, and branches with diversity-inducing user responses for directed exploration.
en.result: DIVERT discovers more failures per token and expands the set of tasks where failures are identified compared to linear rollouts.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers and engineers to evaluate LLM agents more efficiently and comprehensively in multi-turn interactions.

idea_spark.idea: Combine with active learning to automatically identify decision points most likely to expose failures.
idea_spark.risk: Snapshot storage and restoration may introduce overhead; trade-off between granularity and efficiency must be managed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by symbolic execution and path coverage in software testing, adapted to dialogue system evaluation.

papers.title: Addressing Image Authenticity When Cameras Use Generative AI
papers.summary: The ability of generative AI (GenAI) methods to photorealistically alter camera images has raised awareness about the authenticity of images shared online. Interestingly, images captured directly by our cameras are considered authentic and faithful. However, with the increasing integration of deep-learning modules into cameras' capture-time hardware -- namely, the image signal processor (ISP) -- there is now a potential for hallucinated content in images directly output by our cameras. Hallucinated capture-time image content is typically benign, such as enhanced edges or texture, but in certain operations, such as AI-based digital zoom or low-light image enhancement, hallucinations can potentially alter the semantics and interpretation of the image content. As a result, users may not realize that the content in their camera images is not authentic. This paper addresses this issue by enabling users to recover the 'unhallucinated' version of the camera image to avoid misinterpretation of the image content. Our approach works by optimizing an image-specific multi-layer perceptron (MLP) decoder together with a modality-specific encoder so that, given the camera image, we can recover the image before hallucinated content was added. The encoder and MLP are self-contained and can be applied post-capture to the image without requiring access to the camera ISP. Moreover, the encoder and MLP decoder require only 180 KB of storage and can be readily saved as metadata within standard image formats such as JPEG and HEIC.

ai.tldr: 相机内置生成式AI可能导致图像出现幻觉内容，本文提出一种方法，通过优化MLP解码器和编码器，从相机图像中恢复未添加幻觉的原始版本，仅需180KB元数据即可嵌入JPEG/HEIC格式。
ai.motivation: 相机ISP集成深度学习模块后，AI操作（如数字变焦、低光增强）可能引入语义改变的幻觉内容，用户难以察觉，影响图像真实性。
ai.method: 训练一个图像特定的多层感知机（MLP）解码器与模态特定编码器，在捕获后从相机图像中逆向恢复出幻觉添加前的图像，无需访问相机ISP。
ai.result: 编码器和MLP解码器仅需180KB存储，可作为元数据嵌入标准图像格式（JPEG/HEIC），实现后处理恢复。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户识别并还原相机图像中由AI引入的幻觉内容，避免对图像语义的误解。

idea_spark.idea: 将MLP解码器作为图像元数据的一部分，实现轻量级、可嵌入的逆向恢复方案。
idea_spark.risk: 恢复过程可能无法完全消除所有幻觉，且元数据可能被篡改或丢失。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受图像取证中逆向工程思路启发，将AI处理视为可逆操作，通过元数据保留原始信息。

en.tldr: Cameras with generative AI can introduce hallucinated content; this paper proposes an MLP decoder and encoder to recover the original image before hallucination, requiring only 180 KB of metadata embeddable in JPEG/HEIC.
en.motivation: Deep learning modules in camera ISPs (e.g., AI zoom, low-light enhancement) may alter semantics via hallucinations, undermining image authenticity without user awareness.
en.method: Optimize an image-specific MLP decoder with a modality-specific encoder to reverse hallucinated content post-capture, without accessing the camera ISP.
en.result: The encoder and MLP decoder require only 180 KB storage and can be saved as metadata in standard image formats (JPEG/HEIC).
en.help_to_user: Enables users to recover the authentic version of camera images, avoiding misinterpretation of AI-altered content.

idea_spark.idea: Embed a lightweight MLP decoder as image metadata to enable reversible removal of AI-induced hallucinations.
idea_spark.risk: Recovery may be incomplete, and metadata could be tampered with or lost.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by reverse engineering in image forensics, treating AI processing as a reversible operation via metadata.

papers.title: 2L-LSH: A Locality-Sensitive Hash Function-Based Method For Rapid Point Cloud Indexing
papers.summary: The development of 3D scanning technology has enabled the acquisition of massive point cloud models with diverse structures and large scales, thereby presenting significant challenges in point cloud processing. Fast neighboring points search is one of the most common problems, which is frequently used in model reconstruction, classification, retrieval and feature visualization. Hash function is well known for its high-speed and accurate performance in searching high-dimensional data, which is also the core of the proposed 2L-LSH. Specifically, the 2L-LSH algorithm adopts a two-step hash function strategy, in which the popular step divides the bounding box of the point cloud model and the second step constructs a generalized table-based data structure. The proposed 2L-LSH offers a highly efficient and accurate solution for fast neighboring points search in large-scale 3D point cloud models, making it a promising technique for various applications in the field. The proposed algorithm is compared with the well-known methods including Kd-tree and Octree; the obtained results demonstrated that the proposed method outperforms Kd-tree and Octree in terms of speed, i.e. the time consumption of kNN search can be 51.111% and 94.159% lower than Kd-tree and Octree, respectively. And the RN search time can be 54.519% and 41.840% lower than Kd-tree and Octree, respectively.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于局部敏感哈希的两步哈希函数方法2L-LSH，用于快速点云索引，在kNN和RN搜索上显著快于Kd-tree和Octree。
ai.motivation: 大规模点云模型处理中快速邻域搜索是常见难题，现有方法（如Kd-tree、Octree）速度不足。
ai.method: 采用两步哈希策略：第一步划分点云包围盒，第二步构建基于广义表的数据结构，利用局部敏感哈希实现高效索引。
ai.result: kNN搜索时间比Kd-tree和Octree分别降低51.111%和94.159%；RN搜索时间分别降低54.519%和41.840%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种快速、准确的点云邻域搜索方法，适用于模型重建、分类、检索和特征可视化等任务。

idea_spark.idea: 结合空间划分与哈希表，设计自适应哈希函数以处理非均匀分布的点云数据。
idea_spark.risk: 哈希冲突可能影响精度，需平衡哈希表大小与搜索效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴局部敏感哈希在文本检索中的成功，将其适配到3D点云领域，启发更多跨模态索引方法。

zh.motivation: 大规模点云模型处理中快速邻域搜索是常见难题，现有方法（如Kd-tree、Octree）速度不足。

zh.result: kNN搜索时间比Kd-tree和Octree分别降低51.111%和94.159%；RN搜索时间分别降低54.519%和41.840%。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种快速、准确的点云邻域搜索方法，适用于模型重建、分类、检索和特征可视化等任务。

idea_spark.idea: 结合空间划分与哈希表，设计自适应哈希函数以处理非均匀分布的点云数据。
idea_spark.risk: 哈希冲突可能影响精度，需平衡哈希表大小与搜索效率。

en.tldr: Proposes 2L-LSH, a locality-sensitive hash function-based method for rapid point cloud indexing, significantly outperforming Kd-tree and Octree in kNN and range neighbor search speed.
en.motivation: Fast neighboring points search is a common challenge in processing large-scale point cloud models, and existing methods like Kd-tree and Octree are not fast enough.
en.method: Adopts a two-step hash strategy: first partitions the bounding box of the point cloud, then constructs a generalized table-based data structure using locality-sensitive hashing for efficient indexing.
en.result: kNN search time is 51.111% and 94.159% lower than Kd-tree and Octree, respectively; range neighbor search time is 54.519% and 41.840% lower.
en.help_to_user: Provides a fast and accurate neighboring points search method for point cloud processing tasks such as model reconstruction, classification, retrieval, and feature visualization.

idea_spark.idea: Combine spatial partitioning with hash tables to design adaptive hash functions for non-uniformly distributed point cloud data.
idea_spark.risk: Hash collisions may affect accuracy, requiring a balance between hash table size and search efficiency.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from the success of locality-sensitive hashing in text retrieval, adapting it to 3D point clouds, inspiring more cross-modal indexing methods.

papers.title: HiCrew: Hierarchical Reasoning for Long-Form Video Understanding via Question-Aware Multi-Agent Collaboration
papers.summary: Long-form video understanding remains fundamentally challenged by pervasive spatiotemporal redundancy and intricate narrative dependencies that span extended temporal horizons. While recent structured representations compress visual information effectively, they frequently sacrifice temporal coherence, which is critical for causal reasoning. Meanwhile, existing multi-agent frameworks operate through rigid, pre-defined workflows that fail to adapt their reasoning strategies to question-specific demands. In this paper, we introduce HiCrew, a hierarchical multi-agent framework that addresses these limitations through three core contributions. First, we propose a Hybrid Tree structure that leverages shot boundary detection to preserve temporal topology while performing relevance-guided hierarchical clustering within semantically coherent segments. Second, we develop a Question-Aware Captioning mechanism that synthesizes intent-driven visual prompts to generate precision-oriented semantic descriptions. Third, we integrate a Planning Layer that dynamically orchestrates agent collaboration by adaptively selecting roles and execution paths based on question complexity. Extensive experiments on EgoSchema and NExT-QA validate the effectiveness of our approach, demonstrating strong performance across diverse question types with particularly pronounced gains in temporal and causal reasoning tasks that benefit from our hierarchical structure-preserving design.

ai.tldr: 提出HiCrew，一种层次化多智能体框架，通过混合树结构、问题感知字幕和规划层，解决长视频理解中的时空冗余和因果推理难题。
ai.motivation: 长视频理解面临时空冗余和复杂叙事依赖，现有方法牺牲时间连贯性，且多智能体框架缺乏自适应推理策略。
ai.method: 采用镜头边界检测保留时间拓扑，结合相关性引导层次聚类构建混合树；设计问题感知字幕机制生成精准语义描述；集成规划层动态编排智能体协作。
ai.result: 在EgoSchema和NExT-QA上验证有效，尤其在时间和因果推理任务上表现突出。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解长视频中复杂的因果和时间关系，提升视频问答的准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 将混合树与规划层结合，使多智能体系统能根据问题复杂度动态调整推理路径。
idea_spark.risk: 层次聚类可能引入误差，规划层依赖问题复杂度评估的准确性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类分层次理解长视频的启发，模拟先粗后细的推理过程。

en.tldr: Proposes HiCrew, a hierarchical multi-agent framework that addresses spatiotemporal redundancy and causal reasoning in long-form video understanding via a hybrid tree structure, question-aware captioning, and a planning layer.
en.motivation: Long-form video understanding suffers from spatiotemporal redundancy and complex narrative dependencies; existing methods sacrifice temporal coherence and multi-agent frameworks lack adaptive reasoning.
en.method: Uses shot boundary detection to preserve temporal topology with relevance-guided hierarchical clustering; develops question-aware captioning for intent-driven semantic descriptions; integrates a planning layer to dynamically orchestrate agent collaboration.
en.result: Validated on EgoSchema and NExT-QA, showing strong performance especially in temporal and causal reasoning tasks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand complex temporal and causal relationships in long videos, improving video question answering accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Combining hybrid tree with planning layer enables multi-agent systems to dynamically adjust reasoning paths based on question complexity.
idea_spark.risk: Hierarchical clustering may introduce errors; planning layer relies on accurate question complexity estimation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human hierarchical understanding of long videos, simulating a coarse-to-fine reasoning process.

papers.title: Expanding the extreme-k dielectric materials space through physics-validated generative reasoning
papers.summary: The most technologically consequential materials are often the rarest: they occupy narrow regions of chemical space, obey competing physical constraints, and appear only sparsely in existing databases. High-kappa dielectrics, high-Tc superconductors, and ferromagnetic insulators are to name a few. This scarcity fundamentally limits today's data-driven materials discovery, where machine-learning models excel at interpolation but struggle to generate genuinely new candidates. Here, we introduce DielecMIND, an artificial intelligence framework that reframes materials discovery as a reasoning-driven exploration instead of a database-screening problem. Using high-kappa dielectrics as a data-scarce and technologically stringent test case, DielecMIND combines large-language-model hypothesis generation for the first time with physics validated first-principles calculation to navigate chemical space beyond known compounds. Prior to our work, only 14 experimentally or computationally validated materials with kappa > 150 were known. Our framework discovers and validates 5 new such compounds, expanding this rare-materials class by a remarkable = 35% in a single study. Among them, we find that Ba2TiHfO6 exhibits a dielectric constant of 637, minimal loss at low optical frequencies, and stability up to 800 K. Beyond dielectrics, this work demonstrates a new paradigm for artificial-intelligence-guided discovery: one that generates a small number of physically grounded, experimentally plausible candidates yet measurably expands sparsely populated functional materials spaces. Thus, DielecMIND points toward a general strategy for discovering rare, high-impact functional materials where data scarcity has long constrained progress.

ai.tldr: 提出DielecMIND框架，结合大语言模型假设生成与第一性原理计算，在数据稀缺的高介电常数材料领域发现5种新化合物，将已知κ>150的材料数量扩展35%。
ai.motivation: 高介电常数等极端功能材料在化学空间中极为稀疏，传统数据驱动方法难以生成真正的新候选物。
ai.method: 将大语言模型假设生成与物理验证的第一性原理计算相结合，实现推理驱动的材料探索。
ai.result: 发现5种κ>150的新化合物，其中Ba2TiHfO6介电常数达637、低光学损耗、热稳定性至800 K。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种在数据稀缺条件下发现稀有功能材料的通用AI策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将大语言模型用于生成化学假设，再通过第一性原理计算验证，形成闭环发现流程。
idea_spark.risk: 大语言模型可能产生物理上不合理的假设，需严格的计算验证；计算成本可能较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合语言模型的化学直觉与第一性原理的物理约束，有望加速稀有材料的发现周期。
